back_translation: And David fled from Naioth in Ramah and came and said before Jonathan, 'What have I done? What is my iniquity? And what is my sin before your father, that he seeks my life?'
And he said to him, 'Far be it! You shall not die. Behold, my father does not do anything great or small without uncovering my ear. And why would my father hide this matter from me? It is not so.'

And David swore again and said, 'Your father knows well that I have found favor in your eyes, and he said, Let not Jonathan know this lest he be grieved. However, as the LORD lives and as your soul lives, there is but a step between me and death.'
And Jonathan said to David, 'Whatever your soul says, I will do for you.'
And David said to Jonathan, 'Behold, tomorrow is the new moon, and I should sit with the king to eat. But send me away, and I will hide in the field until the third evening.'
If your father indeed observes my absence, then say, David urgently asked of me to run to Bethlehem his city, because there is a yearly sacrifice there for the whole family.
If he says thus, Good, your servant has peace. But if he is greatly angered, know that evil has been determined by him.

And do steadfast-love with your servant, for into a covenant of the LORD you have brought your servant with you. And if there is iniquity in me, kill me yourself; why would you bring me to your father?
And Jonathan said, 'Far be it from you! For if I indeed know that evil has been determined by my father to come upon you, would I not tell it to you?'
And David said to Jonathan, 'Who will tell me, or what will your father answer you harshly?'
And Jonathan said to David, 'Come, and let us go out to the field.' And they two went out to the field.

And Jonathan said to David, 'The LORD God of Israel — when I have sounded out my father at this time tomorrow or the third day, and behold, good for David — and not then will I send to you and uncover your ear?

So may the LORD do to Jonathan and so may He add, if evil pleases my father against you and I do not uncover your ear and send you away, and you go in peace. And may the LORD be with you, as He has been with my father.
And as long as I am still alive, do not steadfast-love of the LORD with me, that I may not die.
And do not cut off your steadfast-love from with my house forever — not even when the LORD cuts off the enemies of David, each one, from the face of the earth.
And Jonathan made a covenant with the house of David, and may the LORD require it from the hand of David's enemies.
And Jonathan again caused David to swear by his love for him, for he loved him with the love of his soul.
And Jonathan said to him, 'Tomorrow is the new moon, and you will be missed, for your seat will be empty.'
And on the third day you shall go down much, and come to the place where you hid yourself on the day of the deed, and remain beside the stone Ezel.
And I will shoot three arrows to the side, as if to shoot at a target.

And behold, I will send the boy, Go, find the arrows. If I expressly say to the boy, Behold, the arrows are on this side of you, take them and come, for there is peace for you and no danger, as the LORD lives.
But if I say thus to the young man, Behold, the arrows are beyond you, then go, for the LORD has sent you away.
And as for the matter that we have spoken, you and I, behold, the LORD is between me and you forever.
And David hid himself in the field, and the new moon came, and the king sat down to eat bread.
And the king sat on his seat as time after time, by the seat by the wall, and Jonathan stood up, and Abner sat beside Saul, and the place of David was missed.
And Saul said nothing on that day, for he said, 'Something has happened to him — he is unclean — surely he is not clean.'

And it happened, on the next day after the new moon, the second, that the place of David was missed, and Saul said to Jonathan his son, 'Why did the son of Jesse not come, neither yesterday nor today, to the bread?'
And Jonathan answered Saul, 'David urgently asked of me to go as far as Bethlehem.

And he said, Send me away please, for our family has a sacrifice in the city, and my brother has commanded me. And now, if I have found favor in your eyes, please let me slip away and see my brothers. Therefore he has not come to the table of the king.'

And Saul's anger burned against Jonathan, and he said to him, 'Son of a perverse and rebellious woman! Do I not know that you have chosen the son of Jesse to your shame and to the shame of your mother's nakedness?
For all the days that the son of Jesse lives upon the earth, you and your kingdom shall not be established. Now send and bring him to me, for he is a son of death.'
And Jonathan answered Saul his father and said to him, 'Why should he be put to death? What has he done?'
And Saul hurled the spear at him to strike him, and Jonathan knew that it was determined from his father to put David to death.
And Jonathan arose from the table in burning anger, and did not eat bread on the second day of the month, for he was grieved over David, because his father had shamed him.
And it happened in the morning, that Jonathan went out to the field for the appointment of David, and a small boy was with him.
And he said to his boy, 'Run, find please the arrows that I shoot.' The boy ran, and he shot the arrow to make it pass him.
And the boy came to the place of the arrow that Jonathan had shot, and Jonathan called after the boy and said, 'Is not the arrow beyond you?'
And Jonathan called after the boy, 'Quickly, hurry, do not stand!' And the boy of Jonathan picked up the arrows and came to his master.
And the boy did not know anything; only Jonathan and David knew the matter.
And Jonathan gave his equipment to the boy who was with him and said to him, 'Go, bring it into the city.'
The boy went, and David arose from beside the south, and fell on his face to the ground, and bowed three times. And they kissed each other and wept each with his friend — until David exceeded.

And Jonathan said to David, 'Go in peace, for we have sworn — we two — in the name of the LORD, saying, The LORD will be between me and you, and between my seed and your seed forever.' And David arose and departed, and Jonathan went into the city.
